Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive report: ## Frontend Codebase Exploration Report I've thoroughly explored the Changelogue frontend codebase. Here's what I found: ### 1. **Layout Architecture** **Root Layout** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/layout.tsx`): - Single root layout file for the entire application - Uses a **flex layout with sidebar + main content area**: - `` on the left (200px wide, dark background) - Right side contains: `
` (12px height bar) + `
` (scrollable) - All pages are wrapped by this layout - **No nested layouts** exist - only this one root layout **Key files:** - `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx` - Navigation component - `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/layout/header.tsx` - Breadcrumb header component --- ### 2. **Header/Navigation Components** **Global Header** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/layout/header.tsx`): - Renders a **12px-high breadcrumb header** that displays the current page location - Dynamically generates breadcrumbs from URL pathname - Maps route segments to human-readable labels (e.g., "projects" â†’ "Projects") - Filters out UUIDs from the breadcrumb display - Single unified header for all pages **Global Sidebar Navigation** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx`): - Dark themed (dark gray background) - Fixed navigation items: Dashboard, Projects, Releases, Channels, Subscriptions - Shows active indicator on current section --- ### 3. **Page Structure - Top-Level Pages** All top-level pages (`/web/app/*/page.tsx`) include **their own page-level headers**: | Page | Location | Header Type | |------|----------|------------| | **Dashboard** | `/web/app/page.tsx` | No explicit header (just content with StatsCards) | | **Projects** | `/web/app/projects/page.tsx` | âś… Has `

Projects

` + description + "New Project" button (lines 147-168) | | **Releases** | `/web/app/releases/page.tsx` | âś… Has `

Releases

` + project filter dropdown (lines 135-173) | | **Channels** | `/web/app/channels/page.tsx` | âś… Has `

Channels

` + "New Channel" button (lines 54-79) | | **Subscriptions** | `/web/app/subscriptions/page.tsx` | âś… Has `

Subscriptions

` + "New Subscription" button (lines 56-81) | | **Sources** | `/web/app/sources/page.tsx` | âś… Has `

All Sources

` + description (lines 35-39) | --- ### 4. **Header Duplication - Critical Finding** **There IS header duplication:** - **Global header** (from root layout): Renders breadcrumb at top of page (e.g., "Projects", "Releases") - **Page-level headers**: Each page renders its own `

` title section with additional UI (buttons, filters) **Example from Projects page:** - Global header shows: `"Projects"` (breadcrumb) - Page-level section shows: `"Projects"` (h1) + description + "New Project" button This means users see the page title **twice** - once in the thin breadcrumb bar and once as the main page heading. --- ### 5. **Detail Page Structure** Dynamic pages render their content through client components: | Route | Component | Structure | |-------|-----------|-----------| | `/projects/[id]` | `ProjectDetail` | Has internal header zone with title, description, action buttons, and tab navigation (lines 183-287) | | `/releases/[id]` | `ReleaseDetail` | Has back link, title, metadata display, and cards (lines 122-202) | | `/projects/[id]/semantic-releases/[srId]` | `SemanticReleaseDetail` | (Rendered as client component) | | `/projects/[id]/edit` | `ProjectEdit` | (Rendered as client component) | **Key observation:** Detail pages have their own internal headers/titles, NOT relying on the global breadcrumb header. --- ### 6. **Component Organization** ``` /web/components/ â”śâ”€â”€ layout/ â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ header.tsx (global breadcrumb) â”‚ â””â”€â”€ sidebar.tsx (global nav) â”śâ”€â”€ dashboard/ â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ stats-cards.tsx â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ recent-releases.tsx â”‚ â””â”€â”€ activity-feed.tsx â”śâ”€â”€ projects/ â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ project-detail.tsx (has internal header + tabs) â”‚ â”śâ”€â”€ project-edit.tsx â”‚ â””â”€â”€ project-form.tsx â”śâ”€â”€ releases/ â”‚ â””â”€â”€ release-detail.tsx (has internal header) â”śâ”€â”€ semantic-releases/ â”‚ â””â”€â”€ semantic-release-detail.tsx â””â”€â”€ ui/ â”śâ”€â”€ provider-badge.tsx â”śâ”€â”€ version-chip.tsx â”śâ”€â”€ section-label.tsx â””â”€â”€ [...other UI components] ``` --- ### 7. **Summary of Header Issues** **Duplication Points:** 1. **Main list pages** (Projects, Releases, Channels, Subscriptions): - Global breadcrumb header: Shows "Projects", "Releases", etc. - Page-level h1: Duplicates the title again with styling - **Result:** Title appears twice on the page 2. **Detail pages** (ProjectDetail, ReleaseDetail): - Global breadcrumb: Tries to show path like "Projects / [uuid]" (UUID filtered out, just shows "Projects") - Page-level detail header: Shows actual title inside the component - **Result:** Better separation since page-level headers are more specific **No nested layouts found** - all pages share the single root layout. --- ### 8. **Design Observations** - **Breadcrumb header is minimal**: 12px height, just text - **Page-level headers are rich**: Include descriptions, action buttons, filters - Excellent! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive summary report of my findings:

## Release Sorting & Fetching Analysis Report

Based on my complete search of both the frontend and backend codebases, here's a comprehensive summary of how releases are sorted and fetched:

---

### **BACKEND API - Default Sort Order**

**File:** `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/pgstore.go`

All backend queries use **`ORDER BY created_at DESC`** (newest first):

1. **ListReleasesBySource** (line 191-214)
   ```go
   FROM releases WHERE source_id = $1 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3
   ```

2. **ListReleasesByProject** (line 216-242)
   ```go
   FROM releases r JOIN sources s ON r.source_id = s.id
   WHERE s.project_id = $1 ORDER BY r.created_at DESC LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3
   ```

3. **ListSemanticReleases** (line 461-486)
   ```go
   FROM semantic_releases WHERE project_id = $1 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3
   ```

4. **GetSemanticReleaseSources** (line 502-521)
   ```go
   FROM releases r JOIN semantic_release_sources srs ON srs.release_id = r.id
   WHERE srs.semantic_release_id = $1 ORDER BY r.created_at DESC
   ```

5. **GetPreviousRelease** (line 621-637)
   ```go
   WHERE source_id = $1 AND version != $2 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
   ```

6. **ListSourcesByProject** (line 117-143)
   ```go
   FROM sources WHERE project_id = $1 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3
   ```

---

### **API HANDLERS**

**Files:** 
- `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/releases.go`
- `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/semantic_releases.go`

The handlers simply delegate to the store methods and return paginated results. The API endpoints are:
- `GET /sources/{id}/releases` - ListBySource
- `GET /projects/{projectId}/releases` - ListByProject  
- `GET /releases/{id}` - Get single release
- `GET /projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases` - ListSemanticReleases
- `GET /semantic-releases/{id}` - Get single semantic release

---

### **FRONTEND SORTING & FETCHING**

#### **1. Releases Page** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/releases/page.tsx`)

**Behavior:**
- Displays releases with **client-side sorting** applied when "All Projects" filter is selected
- Line 100-103: Sorts results by `created_at` DESC:
  ```typescript
  .sort(
    (a, b) =>
      new Date(b.created_at).getTime() - new Date(a.created_at).getTime()
  )
  ```
- When a specific project is selected, relies on backend ordering

**API Calls:**
- `releasesApi.listByProject(projectId, page, perPage)` - per-project releases
- Concurrently fetches from all projects when "All Projects" is selected, then **re-sorts client-side**

---

#### **2. Recent Releases Dashboard Component** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/dashboard/recent-releases.tsx`)

**Behavior:**
- Line 57-64: Fetches releases from first 10 projects and **sorts client-side** by `created_at` DESC:
  ```typescript
  .sort(
    (a, b) =>
      new Date(b.created_at).getTime() - new Date(a.created_at).getTime()
  )
  .slice(0, 8)  // Takes top 8 most recent
  ```
- Displays 8 most recent releases across the dashboard

**API Calls:**
- `releasesApi.listByProject(projectId, 1)` for each project

---

#### **3. ReleaseChips Component** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/projects/page.tsx`, lines 19-69)

**Behavior:**
- Displays on the Projects page table
- Fetches releases with configurable limit (starts with `CHUNK_SIZE = 3`)
- **No additional sorting** - relies on backend ordering (created_at DESC)
- Shows version chips with "moreâ€¦" button to expand
- Line 22-24:
  ```typescript
  () => releasesApi.listByProject(projectId, 1, limit + 1)
  ```

---

#### **4. Semantic Release Chips Component** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/projects/page.tsx`, lines 71-107)

**Behavior:**
- Displays semantic releases on Projects page
- **No explicit sorting** - relies on backend ordering (created_at DESC)
- Line 75:
  ```typescript
  () => srApi.list(projectId, 1, limit + 1)
  ```

---

#### **5. Semantic Releases List Page** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-releases-list.tsx`)

**Behavior:**
- Line 19: Fetches with default pagination:
  ```typescript
  () => srApi.list(projectId)
  ```
- **No additional sorting** - relies on backend ORDER BY created_at DESC
- Lists semantic releases in the order returned from API

---

#### **6. Project Detail - Semantic Releases Tab** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/projects/project-detail.tsx`, lines 458-499)

**Behavior:**
- Line 100:
  ```typescript
  () => srApi.list(id)
  ```
- **No additional sorting** - relies on backend ordering
- Displays semantic releases with status, version, and report summary

---

### **API CLIENT Functions** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/api/client.ts`)

**Release endpoints:**
- Line 80-82: `releases.listBySource(sourceId, page)`
  ```typescript
  /sources/${sourceId}/releases?page=${page}
  ```

- Line 82-83: `releases.listByProject(projectId, page, perPage)`
  ```typescript
  /projects/${projectId}/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}
  ```

- Line 84-85: `releases.get(id)`

**Semantic Release endpoints:**
- Line 112-113: `semanticReleases.list(projectId, page, perPage)`
  ```typescript
  /projects/${projectId}/semantic-releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}
  ```

- Line 114-115: `semanticReleases.get(id)`

---

### **Summary Table**

| Component | Location | Sort Applied | Sort Order | Method |
|-----------|----------|--------------|-----------|--------|
| Releases Page | `/app/releases/page.tsx` | Yes (all projects) | created_at DESC | Client-side |
| Releases Page | `/app/releases/page.tsx` | No (per-project) | created_at DESC | Backend |
| Recent Releases | `dashboard/recent-releases.tsx` | Yes | created_at DESC | Client-side |
| ReleaseChips | `projects/page.tsx` | No | created_at DESC | Backend |
| SemanticReleaseChips | `projects/page.tsx` | No | created_at DESC | Backend |
| Semantic Releases List | `semantic-releases/semantic-releases-list.tsx` | No | created_at DESC | Backend |
| Project Detail SR Tab | `project-detail.tsx` | No | created_at DESC | Backend |

---

### **Key Findings**

1. **Backend uses consistent ordering:** All queries sort by `created_at DESC` (newest releases first)
2. **Frontend applies additional sorting:** Only when aggregating results from multiple projects (Releases page "All Projects", Recent Releases dashboard)
3. **Components use backend ordering:** Most components rely solely on backend-provided order
4. **No version-based sorting:** Sorting is purely time-based (`created_at`), not semantic version ordering
5. **Pagination defaults:** Most endpoints use `page=1` with varying `per_page` values (default 25 for releases, 15 for releases page display)

### **Key Findings**

1. **Backend uses consistent ordering:** All queries sort by `created_at DESC` (newest releases first)
2. **Frontend applies additional sorting:** Only when aggregating results from multiple projects (Releases page "All Projects", Recent Releases dashboard)
3. **Components use backend ordering:** Most components rely solely on backend-provided order
4. **No version-based sorting:** Sorting is purely time-based (`created_at`), not semantic version ordering
5. **Pagination defaults:** Most endpoints use `page=1` with varying `per_page` values (default 25 for releases, 15 for releases page display)

The issue is clear. Everything sorts by `created_at` (when the release was polled/ingested) instead of `released_at` (actual release date). This needs to be fixed in both the backend queries and the frontend client-side sorts.
